items.stimulus_excerpt: Research has shown that blue light emitted from screens can affect sleep patterns by disrupting the body's natural circadian rhythm. Consequently, experts recommend minimizing screen exposure before bedtime. However, given that many people rely on screens for evening activities such as reading or watching videos, it's challenging to implement this advice. As a result, using blue light filtering technologies or glasses can be a practical solution.

items.anomaly: Stimulus only; no numbered claim breakdown follows. Excluded from partition by Step 1 remediation.

items.topic: Michael Jordan vs LeBron James / basketball GOAT
